sections.vendors: M&S publishes an Interactive Supplier Map at corporate.marksandspencer.com (OSH contributor ID: 10061). Key sourcing countries remain India, Sri Lanka, Turkey, Bangladesh and China. No new supplier additions or removals announced this week.
sections.sustainability: M&S is close to achieving 100% sustainably sourced cotton — 100% of cotton across all M&S clothing is responsibly sourced, with the majority through the Better Cotton Initiative. M&S denim is now manufactured with 86% less water compared to industry standard. Plan A 2030 focuses on decarbonizing the value chain, responsible sourcing, and designing waste out of the system.
sections.sourcing: M&S continues its India and Sri Lanka-first sourcing approach for womenswear and lingerie, with Turkey key for knitwear and denim. The brand aims to extend the life of 5 million clothing items through repair, resale and recycling under Plan A for Another Life.
sections.strategy: M&S launched 'The Love That Drop' — a new faster-to-market trend-led capsule programme designed to deliver in-season trend response within shorter lead times. This is a significant strategy shift for M&S, historically known for planned seasonal buys with long lead times. The programme signals M&S buyers will increasingly require agile supplier relationships.
sections.pricing: M&S maintains its premium positioning relative to Primark/George/TU with a strategy of 'quality worth paying for.' No price changes announced. The Love That Drop capsule programme will test whether M&S customers respond to trend-led drops at M&S price points (typically £25–£65).
sections.launches: The Love That Drop — M&S's new faster-to-market trend capsule programme — is now live. First collections include womenswear denim with 86% less water in production and BCI cotton sourcing. New womenswear denim collections feature 100% recycled plastic bottle polyester content.
sections.gossip: M&S's 'Love That Drop' programme is being watched closely by industry insiders — M&S has historically been slow to market on trends, so this is a genuine cultural shift in the buying office. Some supplier sources suggest buyers are still learning how to execute faster turnaround, leading to teething issues with shorter lead time briefs.
soWhat: The Love That Drop faster-to-market programme means M&S buyers will now place smaller orders with shorter lead times — factories need to offer agile production capacity of 500–3,000 units with 8–12 week lead times to participate.

86% water reduction on denim signals M&S is actively specifying responsible manufacturing processes, not just material certifications — start documenting and communicating your factory's water consumption data.

Plan A 2030 circular fashion targets mean M&S will increasingly expect suppliers to offer take-back or recycling programmes for end-of-life garments — early movers on circularity will have an advantage in M&S supplier reviews.
